notes_md:
**ho** = to cough, or a cough. It works as both a verb and a noun.

As a verb, it often goes with words showing frequency or duration: *ho nhiều, ho suốt đêm*. As a noun, you’ll hear *cơn ho* (a coughing fit) or *bị ho* (to have a cough).

Common pairings: *ho khan* (dry cough), *ho có đờm* (phlegmy cough), *ho ra máu* (cough up blood).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I have a cough a lot so my mom took me to see the doctor.
I took medicine but I still have a cough at night.
She is worried when her child has a cough continuously for three days.
exampleSentences: Em bị ho nhiều nên mẹ đưa em đi khám bác sĩ.
Anh uống thuốc nhưng vẫn còn ho vào buổi tối.
Chị lo lắng khi con nhỏ bị ho liên tục suốt ba ngày.
